item.title: Version 0.1.0
item.description: initial release — macOS dictation with dictation, ask, and quick action modes

item.title: Version 0.1.1
item.description: Quick action UX, Sparkle auto-updates, dead code cleanup, lowercase naming

item.title: Version 0.1.2
item.description: Fix M1 Pro sound crash + QuickAction card edge overflow

item.title: Version 0.1.3
item.description: Fix crash on launch for non-build machines (Bundle.module issue)

item.title: Version 0.1.4
item.description: Refreshed app icon and menu bar icon to match the overlay waveform.

item.title: Version 0.1.5
item.description:
Bug fixes and polish.
• Voice isolation / mic indicator now clears immediately when a recording session ends — previously the engine stayed warm for the entire app lifetime. • Fresh AVAudioEngine per session; simpler lifecycle, no lingering hardware allocation. • Fixed cancel-while-starting race that could leave an orphan engine running if the hotkey was released during engine setup. • Start sound no longer gets cut off by voice-processing setup — it now plays after the engine is ready. • ASR model download progress bar is smooth instead of only showing 1% → 49% → 50% → done. • LLM result card height reduced ~20% — more compact without being cramped. • Removed the "Apple Silicon" tag from the speech model list (it was redundant).

item.title: Version 0.1.6
item.description: Add onboarding flow and extract UI theme components (TolkBadge, tolkCaption, tolkSecondary, tolkPrimary)

item.title: Version 0.1.7
item.description: Bug fixes and UI improvements

item.title: Version 0.1.8
item.description: Single unified hotkey — routes to Quick Action when text is selected, Dictation otherwise. Removes separate Ask hotkey. Design system polish: frosted glass overlays, consistent button styles, shared animation tokens.

item.title: Version 0.1.9
item.description: Settings redesign: Dictation and Quick Actions are now separate pages in the sidebar, making it easier to find and configure each feature independently. The Quick Action voice instruction prompt is now user-editable. Plain text mode removed.

item.title: Version 0.1.10
item.description:
Patch: text selection, overlay sizing, test fixes.
• Text selection now works reliably on all machines — Cmd+C fallback uses clipboard polling (up to 300ms) instead of a fixed 100ms wait that was too short for Chrome on slower systems. • Fixed QuickAction card sizing corruption when retriggering the hotkey — chip row overflow during the result→recording resize animation. • Fixed 12 broken tests from prior refactors; integration test suite now compiles (test count 237→241).

item.title: Version 0.1.11
item.description:
Fix: text selection now works in Chrome web content, Slack, Claude desktop, and other Chromium/Electron apps.
• Activates the accessibility tree (AXEnhancedUserInterface / AXManualAccessibility) for Chrome and Electron apps before querying selected text. Previously only worked on machines where something else had already activated Chrome's AX tree.

item.title: Version 0.1.12
item.description:
Text selection: delegate to SelectedTextKit's full strategy chain for broader app compatibility.
• Replaced custom Cmd+C fallback with SelectedTextKit's shortcut strategy (5ms polling, 400ms timeout, muted alert, robust clipboard handling). • Kept fast AX query + Chrome/Electron AX activation as first steps. • Should improve detection on apps where our custom Cmd+C was failing.

item.title: Version 0.1.13
item.description:
Fix: text selection now works universally — Chrome, Slack, Claude desktop, Numbers, and all other apps.
• Root cause: the unified hotkey used a fast AX-only check that skipped the full fallback chain (AX tree activation, Edit→Copy menu action, Cmd+C simulation). Apps without native AX text support always fell through to dictation. • Now: fast AX peek first, then full fallback chain before deciding. ~500ms delay in the "no selection" case for non-native-AX apps; instant for native apps. • Added detailed diagnostic logging ([SEL] [DIAG]) for future debugging.

item.title: Version 0.1.14
item.description:
Codebase cleanup: remove dead code and stale artifacts from prior refactors.
• Deleted 4 orphaned files (RecordingMode, EmbeddingService, unimplemented Firebase plan) • Removed 5 dead AppSettings fields, 4 dead properties/methods • Simplified text insertion to always use clipboard paste • Updated CLAUDE.md architecture docs to match current state

item.title: Version 0.1.15
item.description: Quick Action result buttons now match chip style. Faster hotkey routing: instant AX peek for native apps, AX tree activation for Chrome/Electron (≤200ms), skips slow Cmd+C path entirely.

item.title: Version 0.1.16
item.description:
Fix: text selection in Slack and Terminal now routes to Quick Action correctly.
• Adds menuAction (Edit→Copy via AX menu) to the fast routing path. Apps that don't expose AX selection but have a working Edit menu (Slack, Terminal, Discord, etc.) are now detected. • Dictation overlay still appears within ~250ms when no text is selected — fast-rejects via menu-disabled check.

item.title: Version 0.1.17
item.description: Reliability hardening, performance fixes, improved AI prompts

item.title: Version 0.1.18
item.description: Fix QuickAction dismiss outcome tracking

item.title: Version 0.1.19
item.description: E2E test coverage for coordinator flows and selection routing

item.title: Version 0.1.20
item.description: Real E2E integration tests for ASR, LLM, Dictation and Quick Action modes

item.title: Version 0.1.21
item.description: Fix hotkey display label for non-US keyboard layouts (AZERTY, Dvorak, etc.)

item.title: Version 0.1.22
item.description: Add Logs viewer in Settings for easy bug reporting, fix hotkey labels for non-US keyboards

item.title: Version 0.1.23
item.description: Fix hotkey partial-release stuck state, History freeze, add diagnostic logs

item.title: Version 0.1.24
item.description: Remove press-and-hold mode — toggle is the only behavior now (simpler, more reliable)

item.title: Version 0.1.25
item.description: Auto-send by holding the hotkey on stop (configurable threshold)

item.title: Version 0.1.26
item.description: Onboarding fixes + fix hotkey autorepeat leaking characters during long-press

item.title: Version 0.1.27
item.description:
**Reliability + hardening release.**
- Live model-load progress on first run; no more frozen overlay - Empty LLM result shows a proper error card with Retry instead of blank Copy/Replace - Hotkey recorder rejects system-reserved combos (⌘Q, ⌘Space, ⌘Tab, ⌘⇧3/4/5, ⌘⌥Esc, …) with inline feedback - Default prompt profile protected from deletion; provider selection auto-resets when the active provider is deleted - Keychain API-keys file: chmod before atomic rename closes a brief world-readable window - ASR model switches mid-load cancel the prior prepare cleanly; downloads have a 30-minute hard deadline - Hotkey re-registration during an active session now serialises against cancel - VAD buffer bounded so a stalled drain can't runaway-allocate - Many smaller robustness, UX, and consistency fixes - Build is now warning-free; all 280 tests pass

item.title: Version 0.1.28
item.description:
**UI consistency fix.**
- Logs tab now uses the same Form + grouped-sections layout as General, AI, and Quick Actions — no more jarring layout jump on tab switch - AI settings page title fixed (was stale "Dictation" after the sidebar rename in v0.1.27)

item.title: Version 0.1.29
item.description: Vocabulary for AI post-processing: add terms (with optional hints) that the LLM applies contextually during cleanup. Faster Logs panel for large session logs.

item.title: Version 0.1.30
item.description:
**Vocabulary polish + system prompt fine-tuning.**
Vocabulary - Per-entry on/off toggle removed — delete is the cleaner verb for entries you don't want - "AI off" banner now reacts live to the master toggle right above it - Soft cap (50 entries) is visible: Add Term disables at the cap with an inline note - Long hints are truncated at 200 chars in the rendered prompt; the editor shows a live counter - Save/delete only fires its UI-update notification when the disk write actually succeeded — no more ghost rows
System prompts - Cleaner, halved dictation prompt with the same anchors: transcript-as-content, fillers-out, voice preserved, URLs and dictated punctuation intact - All five Quick Action chips share the same scaffold (task → one example → identical output-contract footer) so the model sees a consistent format - New "Restore Defaults" button in the prompt list to adopt the new built-in text without losing your other profiles
Eval harness - New cross-provider prompt eval suite covers every built-in prompt against Apple Intelligence and (when a key is configured) Gemini - Property-based assertions catch real prompt regressions while tolerating LLM jitter
268 prior tests + 10 new cross-provider evals all passing.

item.title: Version 0.1.31
item.description:
**Cleaner reveal + auto-Enter cue.**
- Dictation overlay now does a deliberate double-pulse on appear (two slow glows separated by a 1-second gap), much easier to spot in peripheral vision than the previous quick fade - Long-press auto-Enter feedback redesigned: replaced the "progress ring" (which read as loading-cut-off when released early) with a green halo that appears only when you cross the threshold. Below threshold: nothing visible — the recording breath does the "still listening" job - The green halo persists through processing so the success cue carries through the entire flow - Smoother halo arrival (300 ms ease-out vs the previous 80 ms snap), no scale effect (sides + top/bottom of the pill now arrive together) - "Loading" / "Loading 42%" instead of "Loading speech model…" on first-run model download - Reduce Motion respected: halo and breath skipped, threshold progress kept (it's information, not motion) - Internal cleanups: clock unification, bounded TimelineViews, cached threshold without lock, several smaller reliability tweaks
279 tests passing.

item.title: Version 0.1.32
item.description: Halo polish — the green auto-Enter confirmation no longer gets shaved at the top and bottom edges of the overlay.
